017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonl;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
027
028/**
029 * Session object that lives for the duration of a single use of {@link JsonlSerializer}.
030 *
031 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
032 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
033 * </ul>
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
036 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonlSupport">JSONL Basics</a>
037 * </ul>
038 */
039@SuppressWarnings({
040   "resource", // Resource management handled externally
041   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable
042   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
043})
044public class JsonlSerializerSession extends JsonSerializerSession {
045
046   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
047
048   /**
049    * Builder class.
050    */
051   @SuppressWarnings({
052      "unused" // ctx passed to super(); parent uses it; Builder stores for constructor chain
053   })
054   public static class Builder extends JsonSerializerSession.Builder<Builder> {
055
056      private JsonlSerializer ctx;
057
058      /**
059       * Constructor
060       *
061       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
062       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
063       */
064      protected Builder(JsonlSerializer ctx) {
065         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
066         this.ctx = ctx;
067      }
068
069      @Override
070      public JsonlSerializerSession build() {
071         return new JsonlSerializerSession(this);
072      }
073   }
074
075   /**
076    * Creates a new builder for this object.
077    *
078    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
079    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
080    * @return A new builder.
081    */
082   public static Builder create(JsonlSerializer ctx) {
083      return new Builder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * Constructor.
088    *
089    * @param builder The builder for this object.
090    */
091   protected JsonlSerializerSession(Builder builder) {
092      super(builder);
093   }
094
095   /**
096    * Opens a low-level push generator that emits JSONL one structural event at a time, with a
097    * trailing newline after every top-level value, bound to this live session.
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * Same honored/ignored builder properties as {@link JsonSerializerSession#serializeTokens(Object)}
101    * except that <c>useWhitespace</c> is forced to <jk>false</jk> &mdash; pretty-printed JSONL is
102    * not standard.
103    *
104    * @param output The output.
105    * @return A new {@link JsonlTokenWriter}.
106    * @throws IOException If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
107    */
108   @Override /* JsonSerializerSession */
109   public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException {
110      var walk = new PojoWalker.Options(
111         isKeepNullProperties(),
112         isTrimEmptyMaps(),
113         isTrimEmptyCollections(),
114         isSortMaps(),
115         isSortCollections(),
116         isTrimStrings(),
117         getMarshallingContext());
118      var settings = new JsonTokenWriter.Settings(
119         false /* useWhitespace — JSONL is one-record-per-line, no pretty-print */,
120         getMaxIndent(),
121         getQuoteChar(),
122         isEscapeSolidus(),
123         isTrimStrings(),
124         false /* simpleAttrs — JSONL uses RFC-8259 strict per line */,
125         walk,
126         false /* disableObject */);
127      return JsonlTokenWriter.forOutput(output, settings);
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Streaming array-element {@link RecordWriter} for JSONL.
132    *
133    * <p>
134    * Unlike the inherited {@link JsonSerializerSession#serializeArrayRecords(Object)} &mdash; which wraps
135    * the writes in a bracketed top-level <c>[...]</c> array and is invalid for line-delimited JSONL &mdash;
136    * this aliases the JSONL line record stream: the {@link JsonlTokenWriter} returned by
137    * {@link #serializeTokens(Object)} is itself a {@link RecordWriter} whose
138    * {@link RecordWriter#write(Object) write(...)} emits one top-level value per line (no surrounding
139    * brackets).  Memory is O(1) in the number of records.
140    *
141    * @param output The output.
142    * @return A new line-delimited {@link RecordWriter}.
143    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
144    */
145   @Override /* ArrayRecordWritable */
146   public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
147      return serializeTokens(output);
148   }
149
150   @Override /* Overridden from JsonSerializerSession */
151   protected void doSerialize(SerializerPipe out, Object o) throws IOException, SerializeException {
152      var w = getJsonWriter(out);
153      var cm = getClassMetaForObject(o);
154      var depth = getInitialDepth();
155
156      if (cm != null && (cm.isCollection() || cm.isArray())) {
157         var c = cm.isArray() ? toList(cm.inner(), o) : (Collection<?>) o;
158         var elementType = cm.getElementType();
159         for (Object item : c) {
160            serializeAnything(w.i(depth), item, elementType, "root", null);
161            w.w('\n');
162         }
163      } else if (cm != null && cm.isStreamable()) {
164         var elementType = cm.getElementType();
165         forEachStreamableEntry(o, cm, item -> {
166            serializeAnything(w.i(depth), item, elementType, "root", null);
167            w.w('\n');
168         });
169      } else {
170         serializeAnything(w.i(depth), o, getExpectedRootType(o), "root", null);
171         w.w('\n');
172      }
173   }
174}
